lead: Your new **Bitcoin Manor** site is **live**. We rebuilt your homepage as a fast, static brand site — using **your own product photography**, pulled straight from your existing store — and it links directly into your shop for every purchase, so **nothing about your checkout changes.**

On Google's PageSpeed test it went from **67 → 85** on mobile, earned an **A for accessibility**, added proper search structured data, and now has a working contact form your old page didn't. It's a one-time build — **you own every account.** Everything you need is below.
ownershipTitle: What You Own & How To Take It
ownershipIntro: Right now both accounts sit under our setup email so we could build it for you. Transfer is by invite — never a shared password.

accounts.name: Hosting — Netlify
accounts.own: → invite your email as Admin, we step off

accounts.name: CMS — Storyblok (free Starter plan)
accounts.own: → invite your email as collaborator, reset password

accounts.name: Domain & store — bitcoinmanor.com
accounts.own: → unchanged; point it at the new site when ready (below)

editSteps: Sign in to [app.storyblok.com](https://app.storyblok.com) with your email (after the invite above).
Open the **Bitcoin Manor** space → the **bitcoin-manor** story.
Edit any text, price, link, image, or the product cards — sections can be added, reordered, or removed.
Click **Publish** (not just Save). See the next box about edits going live.
Use **History** to restore any earlier version.

finishingSteps.title: 1 · Make Storyblok edits go live automatically
finishingSteps.body: Today the site is a published build, so an edit shows up after a quick redeploy (Netlify → Deploys → “Trigger deploy”). To make Publish auto-update the site in ~1 minute, we connect the site’s repo + a build hook + a Storyblok webhook — ~10 minutes, then it’s hands-off.

finishingSteps.title: 2 · Get emailed when someone uses the form
finishingSteps.body: Messages are already saved in Netlify → Forms. To also be emailed, go to Forms → Settings & notifications → Add notification → Email, and enter the inbox you want (e.g. support@bitcoinmanor.com). We left the destination for you to choose.

goingLive.intro: The site is on a free `*.netlify.app` address so you can review it. When you’re ready, the cleanest option:
options: Point **bitcoinmanor.com** (or **www**) at the new site, and move the WooCommerce store to **shop.bitcoinmanor.com** that the “Shop” buttons link to.
Or keep the store where it is and launch the new site on a subdomain.
goingLive.note: In Netlify: Domain management → Add a domain → follow the DNS steps. HTTPS is automatic.

notes: **Real photos, your own.** The hero, the Matrix shot, and the product cards use your existing images — swap any of them in Storyblok.
**No fake details.** We didn’t invent a phone number or address; the trust signals are real photos, reviews, and your socials. Add click-to-call anytime if you want a public number.
**Honest on speed.** We quote the PageSpeed score (67→85) and the move to a fast static stack — not a “seconds” figure, since Google’s lab numbers aren’t real-user data.
